title: Gemini CLI Agentic Benchmark Task
description: Schema for a lifecycle benchmark task that requires requirements elicitation, implementation planning, red-green TDD, and runtime validation.

commit_sha.description: Full 40-character commit SHA to pin the task to.

feature_request.description: Deliberately underspecified feature request. Must contain ambiguities that require clarification.

phase_1_expected_questions.description: Ambiguities the agent should surface during requirements elicitation.

phase_1_answers.description: Canonical answers to expected questions, keyed by question/ambiguity topic.

phase_2_expected_plan_components.description: Required elements the implementation plan must address.

phase_3_test_file.description: Primary test file path that must fail before implementation.

phase_3_test_paths.description: Test file paths the agent is expected to create in phase 3a. Runner verifies these exist before running the verification command.

phase_3_verification_command.description: Shell command to run tests. Must exit non-zero before implementation, zero after.

phase_4_runtime_check.description: Description of the runtime state to validate in phase 4.

phase_4_runtime_type.description: Type of runtime validation. interactive_server: agent starts a server and hits endpoints. build_and_test: agent runs build/tests and interprets output. browser_mcp: agent uses browser MCP tooling (future work).

setup_commands.description: Commands to run sequentially in the cloned repo directory before handing off to the agent.

setup_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time in seconds for all setup commands to complete. Defaults to 120.

min_tool_calls.description: Minimum total tool calls across all phases. Tasks must require at least 3 sequential dependent tool calls by design.

failure_mode_category.description: The primary failure mode this task is designed to catch. tool_sequence_error is a design-time label only and never appears in runtime results.

references.description: Benchmark references this task relates to (e.g., SWE-bench, ARC-AGI-3).
